pi_items.source: Confirmed via task description ('A tiddler named Triage Dashboard'), export_result.sh line 12 which checks tiddler_exists 'Triage Dashboard', and the description which requires this exact title.

Confirmed via task description ('$:/custom/TriageAssessment'), export_result.sh line 29 which searches for files matching '*custom*TriageAssessment*'. The $: prefix is a TiddlyWiki system tiddler convention.
Confirmed via task description ('Waiting Room Board'), export_result.sh line 37 which checks tiddler_exists 'Waiting Room Board'.
Confirmed via task description which specifies form inputs 'bound to $:/temp/NewPatient'. export_result.sh line 89 greps for this exact string in the dashboard body text.

Confirmed via setup_task.sh which creates 'Intake Form - Maria Santos.tid' with field 'patient-name: Maria Santos'. Verified in live environment: tiddler appeared in API tiddler list (raw_api_tiddler_list.txt) and was accessible via browser (screenshot 03_maria_santos_intake_rendered.png).

Independently verified against ESI (Emergency Severity Index) clinical guidelines. Maria Santos presents with acute chest pain radiating to left arm (classic acute coronary syndrome/MI presentation), tachycardia (HR 112), fever (39.8C), hypertension (165/95), diaphoresis. Per ESI Handbook (AHRQ): chest pain radiating to left arm is explicitly cited as an ESI Level 2 (Emergent) example — 'high-risk situation, requiring rapid evaluation within 10 minutes.' Per CTAS: Level 2 = Emergent = 'potential threat to life requiring rapid medical interventions' including 'sudden sharp chest pains.' Level 2 is correct.

Task description specifies registered patients get tags 'Patient' and 'WaitingRoom'. The task instructions say 'Move Aisha Patel to Treatment' — Maria Santos is NOT moved, so she retains WaitingRoom tag. Confirmed by cross-referencing export_result.sh which checks has_patient_tag and has_waitingroom_tag for each patient.

Confirmed via setup_task.sh which creates 'Intake Form - James Wilson.tid' with field 'patient-name: James Wilson'. Verified in live environment: tiddler appeared in API tiddler list and was readable with correct fields (age: 34, chief-complaint: Left ankle injury).

Independently verified against ESI guidelines. James Wilson presents with ankle sprain from basketball — normal vitals (37.1C, HR 78, BP 122/78), no high-risk features, hemodynamically stable, needs only imaging (X-ray). Per ESI Handbook: 'ankle sprain needing one resource (X-ray) = ESI Level 4 (Less Urgent).' Per CTAS: Level 4 = Less Urgent. Level 4 is correct.
Same logic as Maria Santos — James Wilson is not moved to Treatment per the task instructions, so retains Patient + WaitingRoom tags.

Confirmed via setup_task.sh which creates 'Intake Form - Aisha Patel.tid' with field 'patient-name: Aisha Patel'. Verified in live environment: tiddler appeared in API tiddler list and was rendered correctly in browser (screenshot 04_aisha_patel_intake_rendered.png).

Independently verified against ESI and CTAS clinical guidelines plus medical literature. Aisha Patel presents with thunderclap headache ('worst headache of my life'), sudden onset reaching maximum intensity within seconds, neck stiffness (meningismus), photophobia, visual disturbances, vomiting, severely elevated BP (180/110), family history of cerebral aneurysm. Per StatPearls (NCBI NBK560629): thunderclap headache is a medical emergency, with SAH present in ~25% of cases. This presentation (thunderclap + meningismus + family history of aneurysm) has extremely high pretest probability for subarachnoid hemorrhage, which has 30-50% mortality. Per CTAS: Level 1 = Resuscitation = 'conditions that are a threat to life requiring immediate aggressive interventions.' Per ESI: Level 1 = immediate life-saving intervention required. Level 1 is correct.

Task description explicitly states 'Move Aisha Patel to Treatment.' The ViewTemplate's Move to Treatment button is designed to remove the WaitingRoom tag and add the Treatment tag. After processing, Aisha should have Patient + Treatment (not WaitingRoom). Confirmed by cross-referencing export_result.sh which checks has_treatment_tag and has_waitingroom_tag.

privileged_info_summary: This task operates on 3 synthetic patient intake forms created by setup_task.sh in a TiddlyWiki instance. The agent must build three interacting tiddlers: (1) 'Triage Dashboard' tagged 'Dashboard' — a registration form with 6 input fields (patient-name, age, chief-complaint, temperature, heart-rate, blood-pressure) bound to state tiddler '$:/temp/NewPatient', with a 'Register Patient' button that creates Patient+WaitingRoom-tagged tiddlers with triage-status 'Awaiting Triage' and clears the temp tiddler; (2) '$:/custom/TriageAssessment' tagged '$:/tags/ViewTemplate' — shows on Patient-tagged tiddlers, displaying a vitals table and 5 triage buttons (1-Resuscitation, 2-Emergent, 3-Urgent, 4-Less Urgent, 5-Non-Urgent) that each set triage-level and triage-status to 'Triaged', plus a 'Move to Treatment' button that swaps WaitingRoom tag for Treatment tag; (3) 'Waiting Room Board' tagged 'Dashboard' — two dynamic sections listing WaitingRoom patients by triage-status with triaged patients sorted by triage-level ascending. After building, the agent must register all 3 patients from their IntakeForm tiddlers, assign correct triage levels (Maria Santos: Level 2 — chest pain/possible MI, James Wilson: Level 4 — ankle sprain, Aisha Patel: Level 1 — thunderclap headache/possible SAH), and move Aisha Patel to Treatment. Final expected state: Maria Santos tiddler exists with tags Patient+WaitingRoom and triage-level '2'; James Wilson exists with tags Patient+WaitingRoom and triage-level '4'; Aisha Patel exists with tags Patient+Treatment (NOT WaitingRoom) and triage-level '1'. The intake forms contain realistic clinical data: Maria Santos (67F, temp 39.8, HR 112, BP 165/95, chest pain), James Wilson (34M, temp 37.1, HR 78, BP 122/78, ankle injury), Aisha Patel (45F, temp 37.4, HR 96, BP 180/110, thunderclap headache with meningismus and family history of cerebral aneurysm). The wiki starts with 22 tiddlers total (19 seed + 3 intake forms). TiddlyWiki runs on port 8080 in Firefox at http://localhost:8080/.
